title: Production-readiness roadmap
notes: Phases 1-3 are independent by artifacts and may be dispatched to parallel agents.
Phase 5 intentionally depends on auth (phase 1): scoping the SSE feed is meaningless without an identity to scope to.

phases.title: Authentication & authorization boundary
phases.goal: Replace the persona toggle with a real identity: employees file only for themselves, managers decide only for their reports, enforced server-side (TRD §13.3-1).

tasks.description: Write failing route-handler tests: unauthenticated requests 401; employee filing for another employee 403; non-manager decision 403.
tasks.verification: pnpm test (new auth tests red first, then green)

tasks.description: Add session middleware (mock OIDC for demo: signed cookie with role+employeeId) and enforce in route handlers.
Remove persona links from landing; derive view from session; update e2e to authenticate.

acceptance: No route handler trusts client-supplied employeeId without matching the session
TRD §12/§13 updated: the toggle caveat is removed

phases.title: Persistent session ledger
phases.goal: Survive reloads: persist the pending overlay (Zustand persist → localStorage) so an employee's in-flight requests outlive a refresh (TRD §11 known limitation).

tasks.description: Failing test: ledger rehydrates from storage; contradicted/pre-confirmation phases reconcile against HCM records on boot (a request that verified while we were away must not double-hold).
Add zustand/persist to requestLedger with a boot-time reconciliation pass (reuse syncDecisions + verification reads).
E2E: file → reload → request still visible with correct phase.

acceptance: Reload mid-flight never double-counts a hold (projection invariant holds across rehydration)

phases.title: Write idempotency
phases.goal: Client retries after ambiguous failures cannot double-file: filings carry an idempotency key the mock HCM deduplicates on (TRD §13.3-4).

tasks.description: Failing store test: two fileRequest calls with the same idempotency key return the same record and debit once.
Thread the client request id (already a UUID) as the idempotency key through hcmApi, wire.ts and both transports.
Revisit submitFlow's hcm_silent path: with idempotency, retry-after-silence can become safe and automatic for ONE attempt; update FSM tests and TRD §6.5 accordingly.

acceptance: Duplicate submissions are structurally impossible, not just unlikely

phases.title: Demo-surface gating
phases.goal: Chaos header, reset and anniversary trigger are env-gated: enabled in dev/test/demo, absent in production builds (TRD §13.3-3).

tasks.description: Failing route tests: with HCM_TEST_SURFACES!=1, reset/trigger return 404 and x-chaos is ignored.
Gate in routeHelpers + reset/trigger handlers; e2e/CI/Vercel demo set the env explicitly.
tasks.verification: pnpm test:e2e (env set) + manual: prod build without env hides surfaces

acceptance: A production deployment without the flag exposes no test surface

phases.title: Scoped realtime + transport hardening
phases.goal: SSE feed filtered to the authenticated user's visibility; rate limiting on writes; CSP/CORS headers (TRD §13.3-2/5).

tasks.description: Failing test: employee session receives only its own cells/requests over SSE; manager receives their reports'.
Filter events server-side by session in the SSE route; add basic rate limit to POST/PATCH handlers.
next.config: CSP and CORS headers; verify nothing breaks Storybook/e2e.
tasks.verification: pnpm build && pnpm test:e2e && pnpm test:storybook

acceptance: No user can observe another user's balances through the event feed

phases.title: Observability of contradictions
phases.goal: The system's health signal IS its contradiction rate: count verify-mismatch/conflict/silent events, expose a /api/metrics endpoint and surface trends (a spike means HCM drift or a bug).

tasks.description: Failing test: submitFlow outcomes increment typed counters (confirmed/contradicted-by-reason/denied).
Minimal metrics module + endpoint; document alert thresholds in TRD §11.

acceptance: A silent-failure storm is visible in metrics before users report it
